expectations.text: Output shows all 3 stages: advocate defense, critic challenge, and arbiter ruling
expectations.evidence: Eval-Advocate Report, Eval-Critic Report, Eval-Arbiter Report — all present

expectations.text: Advocate assesses each of the 5 acceptance criteria as PASS or ACKNOWLEDGE_GAP
Advocate scores 4 dimensions: Functionality, Correctness, Design Fidelity, Code Quality
expectations.evidence: Dimension scores table: Functionality 7, Correctness 6, Design Fidelity 7, Code Quality 5

expectations.text: Critic identifies the missing edge case tests as a gap
expectations.evidence: C-001: Criterion 5 not met — happy path only, missing validation failure, duplicate email, malformed request, boundary tests

expectations.text: Critic identifies the DB column name exposure in error messages
expectations.evidence: C-002: Error messages expose internal DB column names — security risk and correctness problem
Arbiter scores: Functionality 7, Correctness 5, Design Fidelity 6, Code Quality 4 — with dispute resolutions

expectations.text: Verdict is one of: PASS, NEEDS_CHANGES, or REGRESSION_DETECTED
Dimension scores are compared against thresholds (Functionality: 7, Correctness: 7, Design Fidelity: 6, Code Quality: 5)
expectations.evidence: Thresholds shown in table: Functionality MET (7/7), Correctness NOT MET (5/7), Design Fidelity MET (6/6), Code Quality NOT MET (4/5)
